message:
style: apply rustfmt formatting fixes across decoding, decoder, scheduler, and router

These changes are purely cosmetic formatting adjustments with zero logic
or behavioral impact. They bring four source files into compliance with
the project's rustfmt configuration, continuing the formatting pass that
was started in 865d2bd3.

Specific changes by file:

src/decoding.rs:
- Remove trailing whitespace on line 681 inside decode_block(), where a
  blank line between `padding_rows` computation and the comment block had
  an invisible trailing space character. rustfmt strips these.
- Remove two consecutive blank lines (lines 799-800) between the end of
  decode_block() and the start of seed_for_block(). rustfmt enforces a
  maximum of one blank line between items.

src/raptorq/decoder.rs:
- Reorder imports in the top-level `use` statement from
  `{gf256_add_slice, gf256_addmul_slice, Gf256}` to
  `{Gf256, gf256_add_slice, gf256_addmul_slice}`, placing the type
  alias before the free functions. This matches rustfmt's default
  alphabetical ordering for grouped imports.
- Apply the same alphabetical reordering to the test module's import of
  `test_log_schema` items, moving `validate_unit_log_json` after the
  type names `UnitDecodeStats` and `UnitLogEntry`.
- Reformat five `assert!()` macro invocations in the
  `failure_classification_is_explicit` test. Previously the closing
  parenthesis of each assert was chained onto the method call line
  (e.g., `.is_recoverable());`). rustfmt wraps these so the closing
  paren sits on its own line, improving readability for multi-line
  struct-variant expressions inside assert macros.

src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs:
- Collapse two `trace!()` macro invocations where the string literal
  message was on its own continuation line after `priority,`. rustfmt
  joins these onto a single line with `priority,` since the result fits
  within the line-length limit. This affects the `inject_ready` method
  at both the "injected into global ready queue" and "NOT scheduled"
  trace callsites.

src/transport/router.rs:
- Reorder the `smallvec` crate import from `{smallvec, SmallVec}` to
  `{SmallVec, smallvec}`, placing the type before the macro in
  alphabetical order.
- Swap the order of `use std::sync::Arc` and
  `use std::sync::atomic::{...}` so that `Arc` (shorter path) comes
  after the `atomic` submodule import. Additionally reorder the atomic
  types inside the braces from `{AtomicU32, AtomicU64, AtomicU8, ...}`
  to `{AtomicU8, AtomicU32, AtomicU64, ...}`, sorting by the numeric
  suffix width (8 < 32 < 64) which matches rustfmt's alphabetical
  comparison of the digit characters.

No functional changes. No test changes. No API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
